title: Aira insularis
taxonomy: Aira insularis is classified within the family Poaceae, commonly known as the grass family. It belongs to the order Poales, which is a group of flowering plants that are closely related to the grasses. The genus Aira is a small group of tufted grasses, and Aira insularis is one of its species. Within the Poaceae family, Aira insularis is further classified as a member of the subfamily Pooideae. The classification of Aira insularis provides insight into its evolutionary relationships with other plants.

intro: Aira insularis, also known as the island hairgrass, is a species of flowering plant in the family Poaceae. Traditionally, Aira insularis has been used to treat various health conditions, including fever reduction, anti-inflammatory effects, and antioxidant properties, which can help alleviate symptoms associated with colds, flu, and other respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Aira insularis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Aira insularis is commonly prepared as a tea or infusion, and it is also used in the form of extracts or dried powder in traditional remedies and supplements.
